# Kube-DC Documentation

> Kube-DC is an open-source Kubernetes Data Center platform. It provides multi-tenancy, virtual machines (KubeVirt), managed Kubernetes clusters (Kamaji + Cluster API), managed databases (PostgreSQL, MariaDB), OVN networking with public/floating IPs, S3 object storage, block storage, backups, SSO (Keycloak OIDC), hierarchical RBAC, and billing. Deployed on bare-metal or cloud infrastructure.

For the complete documentation in a single file, see: https://docs.kube-dc.com/llms-full.txt

## Cloud Guide — User Documentation

- [Kube-DC Cloud Overview](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud): What Kube-DC Cloud offers and how to get started
- [What is Kube-DC?](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/what-is-kube-dc): Platform overview and key capabilities
- [Sign Up & Login](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/sign-up-login): Account creation and authentication
- [Dashboard Overview](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/dashboard-overview): Navigating the web console
- [Core Concepts](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/core-concepts): Organizations, projects, and resource hierarchy
- [Creating Your First Project](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/first-project): Project setup wizard
- [CLI & IDE Access](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/cli-kubeconfig): kube-dc CLI tool, kubeconfig, and IDE integration
- [Deploy Your First App](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/deploy-first-app): WordPress deployment tutorial with Helm
- [Deploying VMs](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/creating-vm): How to create virtual machines via UI and kubectl
- [Connecting to Your VM](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/connecting-vm): VNC console, SSH, and network access
- [Managing VM Lifecycle](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/vm-lifecycle): Start, stop, restart, pause, and delete VMs
- [Networking Overview](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/networking-overview): How networking works in Kube-DC
- [External & Floating IPs](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/public-floating-ips): EIP and FIP management
- [VPC & Private Networking](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/private-networking): Project-level VPC isolation
- [Service Exposure Guide](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/service-exposure): Exposing services to the internet
- [Provisioning Managed Kubernetes](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/provisioning-cluster): Creating managed K8s clusters
- [Cluster Management](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/cluster-management): Scaling, upgrading, and managing clusters
- [Managed Databases](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/managed-databases): PostgreSQL and MariaDB as a service
- [Block Storage](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/block-storage): Persistent volumes and DataVolumes
- [Object Storage (S3)](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/object-storage): S3-compatible buckets via Rook Ceph
- [Backups & Snapshots](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/backups-snapshots): Velero-based backup and restore
- [User and Group Management](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/team-management): RBAC, roles, and team management
- [Billing & Usage](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/billing-usage): Subscription plans and resource monitoring
- [Security Restrictions](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/security-restrictions): Tenant isolation and security boundaries
- [AI IDE Integration](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/ai-ide-integration): MCP server for AI coding assistants
- [GitOps & Automation](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/gitops): ArgoCD and Flux integration
- [Examples](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/examples): Ready-to-use YAML examples
- [Tutorials](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/tutorials): Step-by-step guides
- [Community & Support](https://docs.kube-dc.com/cloud/community-support): Slack, GitHub, and support channels

## Platform Docs — Operator Documentation

- [Platform Overview](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform): Deploying and managing Kube-DC on your infrastructure
- [Installation Overview](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/installation-overview): Requirements and installation methods
- [Installation on Hetzner](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/installation-hetzner): Step-by-step Hetzner bare-metal guide
- [Architecture Overview](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/architecture-overview): System components and layers
- [Networking Architecture](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/architecture-networking): OVN and Envoy Gateway internals
- [Multi-Tenancy Architecture](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/architecture-multi-tenancy): Namespace isolation and Keycloak RBAC
- [Virtualization Architecture](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/architecture-virtualization): KubeVirt integration details
- [Security Model](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/security-model): Pod security, network policies, and RBAC
- [Managing OS Images](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/managing-os-images): Adding and configuring VM images
- [Project Resources](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/project-resources): Resource quotas and limits per project
- [Windows VM Setup](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/windows-vm-setup): Configuring Windows virtual machines
- [SSO with Google Auth](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/sso-google-auth): Keycloak Google identity provider setup
- [External Networking](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/networking-external): External network configuration
- [Deploy MetalLB HA](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/deploy-metallb-ha): High-availability MetalLB setup
- [Deploy Rook Ceph Object Storage](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/deploy-rook-ceph-object-storage): S3 storage backend setup
- [Roadmap](https://docs.kube-dc.com/platform/roadmap): Upcoming features and planned improvements
